mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the unemployment rate below 6.0 percent signals labor market tightening that boosts wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive equity returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day forward returns in more than 60 percent of instances where UNRATE drops below 6.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer credit balances combined with flat or declining industrial production indicate a demand-supply mismatch that triggers stagflationary earnings revisions, depressing equity valuations over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 50 percent of observations where RSAFS rises and INDPRO stagnates.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating housing starts alongside expanding real GDP growth signal robust consumer sector momentum, generating positive near-term equity price appreciation during post-shock recovery phases.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 60 percent of quarters where HOUST and GDPC1 both accelerate.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the consumer price index exceeding 0.5 percent forces market pricing of aggressive Federal Reserve tightening, raising discount rates and compressing forward equity valuations.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive in more than 55 percent of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 0.5 percent monthly threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly payroll growth combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms labor market tightening that supports corporate revenue expansion, driving positive equity returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are negative in more than 50 percent of observations where PAYEMS rises and UNRATE falls simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing output and capacity utilization, directly boosting corporate earnings expectations and driving positive near-term equity price momentum.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 60 percent of months where INDPRO increases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer credit balances alongside accelerating inflation signal debt-fueled consumption that will soon face repayment headwinds, pressuring equity valuations during credit expansion phases.
failure_condition: Equity returns are positive in more than 55 percent of months where RSAFS and CPIAUCSL both accelerate.
